intro: Transitioning from Java to C++ moves a developer into a world of zero-cost abstractions, multi-paradigm design, and deterministic resource lifecycle management. While preserving object-oriented concepts, C++ eliminates runtime VM overhead, giving developers full control over memory layout and hardware optimization. This change is vital for systems engineering, game engine development, and low-latency financial systems.

diffs.from: Statically typed, single-root nominal class hierarchy managed safely by runtime VM boundary verifications.
diffs.to: Statically typed, multiple-inheritance class system supporting compile-time templates and undefined behaviors for unverified types.

diffs.from: Strictly structured around garbage-collected class modules with standard dot-notation object references.
diffs.to: Extensive multi-paradigm syntax supporting operator overloading, templates, direct pointer access, and reference semantics.

diffs.from: Automatic runtime garbage collection where objects live exclusively on the managed JVM heap.
diffs.to: Deterministic scope-based lifecycle management (RAII), supporting stack allocation, manual heap management, and smart pointers.

diffs.from: Built-in managed synchronization primitives, monitors, execution pools, and lightweight virtual threads.
diffs.to: Standard thread library (std::thread), futures, promises, and direct access to native operating system threading systems.

diffs.from: Monolithic built-in platform framework providing cross-platform networking, graphics, and data access libraries.
diffs.to: Standard Template Library (STL) heavily optimized for abstract containers, type traits, algorithms, and stream I/O.

examples.title: Deterministic Resource Management (RAII)
examples.description: Converting Java's AutoCloseable interface and try-with-resources statement into a native C++ scoped destructor lifecycle.

examples.title: Generics vs Templates
examples.description: Porting a generic Java utility container to a highly performant, type-instantiated C++ template class.

tips.title: Prefer Stack Over Heap
tips.body: Do not write 'new' for every object instantiation. Allocate objects on the stack by default so they clear automatically when exiting scope.

tips.title: Master Smart Pointers
tips.body: When heap allocation is absolutely necessary, manage ownership safely via std::unique_ptr and std::shared_ptr instead of raw pointers.

tips.title: Avoid Type Erasure Assumptions
tips.body: C++ templates generate actual concrete code variations for every parameter type at compilation, unlike Java Generics which erase type markers.

tips.title: Implement Rule of Three/Five
tips.body: When managing a raw systemic resource, ensure you correctly implement custom copy/move constructors and destructors to prevent double-free crashes.
